title: mallotus moluccanus
intro: Mallotus moluccanus, also known as Riddhi or Pichkari, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia, belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae. The plant is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Mallotus moluccanus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat skin infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, Mallotus moluccanus is often used in the form of decoctions, infusions, or powder, and it is also used in traditional Indian medicine to treat various ailments.
